name: International UAW Federal Credit Union
description_short: Federal credit union serving UAW members and affiliates with online banking, loans, and shared branch access through the CO-OP network.
description_long:
International UAW Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative chartered to serve United Auto Workers members and their families. Based in Detroit, Michigan at 8000 E Jefferson Ave, the credit union has operated since 1936 as a specialized financial institution for the automotive industry workforce. The organization operates under federal NCUA insurance and maintains traditional credit union governance structures prioritizing member benefit over profit maximization.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including checking and savings accounts, credit union loans, online banking with bill pay functionality, mobile banking access, and credit cards. Members benefit from 24/7 automated account access via the Unity-Line phone system (1-800-983-9591), direct deposit services, payroll deduction options, and participation in the CO-OP shared branch network providing access to thousands of locations nationally. The institution recently updated its online banking platform as of April 8, 2024, with enhanced security features requiring SSN verification for login.

International UAW FCU distinguishes itself through exclusive membership tied to UAW employment or family relationships, providing tailored financial products for union workers. The credit union offers discounted insurance programs through TruStage partnerships covering auto, home, life, and accidental death coverage. Operating hours are Monday-Friday, 9:30 AM to 4:30 PM at the main Detroit location, with holiday closings observed. The institution emphasizes member service accessibility through multiple contact channels and comprehensive e-service offerings.

As a credit union, IUAWFCU provides member-owned financial services typically with lower fees and competitive rates compared to traditional banks, though access is restricted to eligible members. The organization's focus on a specific membership demographic (UAW-affiliated workers) means it is not a universal banking option. Members must verify eligibility requirements and navigate membership application procedures to access services.
pros: 24/7 automated account access via Unity-Line phone system with no service fee
Free direct deposit and payroll deduction services for automatic savings and loan payments
Access to thousands of shared branch locations nationwide through CO-OP network beyond main Detroit office
Recently modernized online banking platform (updated April 2024) with enhanced security features
Discounted insurance programs through TruStage partnerships for auto, home, life, and accidental death coverage
Member-owned credit union structure typically offering lower fees and competitive rates than traditional banks
Specialized service for UAW members and families with tailored financial products
cons: Membership restricted to UAW members, retirees, and eligible family members—not open to general public
Limited service hours (Monday-Friday, 9:30 AM to 4:30 PM) at main Detroit location with no weekend availability
Online banking login system requires leading zeros for member numbers under 6 digits, adding user friction
Website lacks detailed information on loan types, APRs, account features, fees, and minimum deposit requirements
No physical branches outside Detroit location (reliant on CO-OP shared branches for in-person service)
best_for: UAW union members and retirees seeking member-owned financial services with competitive rates
Auto industry workers wanting employer-integrated payroll deduction and direct deposit services
Members preferring 24/7 automated account management via phone system over website access
Families of UAW-eligible members seeking group insurance discounts through TruStage programs
diagnosis: International UAW FCU is appropriate for union workers in automotive or related UAW-represented industries seeking member-owned banking with 24/7 phone access and discounted insurance options. Primary caveat: membership is restricted to eligible UAW affiliates, making this unavailable to the general public, and the website provides insufficient detail on specific products, rates, and fees to fully evaluate competitiveness.
services: Online banking with bill pay functionality
Checking and savings accounts (deposit products unspecified on website)
Shared branch access via CO-OP network
Shared ATM access via CO-OP network
TruStage insurance programs (auto, home, life, accidental death)
